race.name: Leadville Loppet
race.tagline: Nordic skiing at 10,000 feet on the Mineral Belt Trail — Leadville's high-altitude loppet tests lungs and legs in Colorado's legendary mining town.

climate.description: Leadville sits at 10,152 feet (3,094m), making this one of the highest-altitude Nordic races in the world. Thin air, intense UV, and variable conditions. Cancelled in 2026 due to insufficient snow.

course.primary: Course on the Mineral Belt Trail circling historic Leadville at 10,000+ feet. Rolling terrain with views of the Sawatch Range and Mount Massive.
features: One of the highest-altitude Nordic races globally
Mineral Belt Trail around historic Leadville
Views of Sawatch Range and Mount Massive

nordic_lab_rating.score_note: Extreme altitude makes this a unique challenge, but inconsistent snow and limited field size constrain the score.

history.summary: The Leadville Loppet has been held since 2003 on the Mineral Belt Trail in Leadville, Colorado — the highest incorporated city in the US at 10,152 feet. The race was cancelled in 2026 due to lack of snow but plans to return in 2027.
notable_facts: One of the highest-altitude Nordic races in the world (10,000+ ft)
Uses the Mineral Belt Trail around historic Leadville
Cancelled in 2026 due to insufficient snow
Leadville is famous for its Leadville 100 ultra events

youtube_data.search_query: Leadville Loppet cross country ski race Leadville

videos.title: The Leadville Loppet Races @ Planet Leadville
videos.description:
The Leadville Loppet is a series of cross-country ski races including a 5K, 10K and 22K sprints along the Mineral Belt Trail in Leadville, Colorado USA.  Funds derived from the participants’ entry fees are used for the care and maintenance of the trail. Show your support for the MBT by competing, volunteering, or by making a donation. The race itself is a real winner! 
Skiers glide through a rolling, forested network of trails, leading racers on to the MBT, where climbs and descends await. The tracks follow along abandoned rail grades left behind from Leadville’s mining era, offering a passing history lesson to the skinny skiers. It’s a one-of-a-kind course which should be laden (and groomed) with fresh snow. The Loppet has also been added to the list of American Birkebeiner qualifiers!  Skiers will be able to utilize results from either the 22 or 44 km race for improving their wave placement for the Birkie or Korte. More details on that can be found HERE: https://www.mineralbelttrail.com/leadville-loppet-1
This video is from the Leadville Today archives, now Planet Leadville. Both offerings are owned by The Great Pumpkin LLC, a digital media company established in 2011. Contact grpkin@gmail.com for use guidelines and licensing.
